stemHtml: What should a pilot do if his/her aircraft is subjected to an act of unlawful interference and contact to an ATS unit cannot be established?

options.a: Proceed at a level which differs from the cruising levels normally used for IFR by 500 ft in an area where a vertical separation minimum of 1000 ft is applied.
options.b: Proceed at a level which differs from the cruising levels normally used for IFR by 1000 ft in an area where a vertical separation minimum of 500 ft is applied.
options.c: Proceed at a level which differs from the cruising levels normally used for IFR by 2000 ft in an area where a vertical separation minimum of 1300 ft is applied.
options.d: Proceed at a level which differs from the cruising levels normally used for IFR by 1000 ft in an area where a vertical separation minimum of 1000 ft is applied.
